files.SKILL.md:
---
name: operational-axioms-for-terminal-work
description: Eleven axioms for terminal agents. A1 path-first, A2 parallel reads, A3 observe-don't-extrapolate, A4 run-don't-read, A5 change mechanism on second failure, A6 exact matching, A7 self-contained scripts, A8 rewrite configs, A9 copy before parsing, A10 file tool for special characters, A11 confirm by read not narration.
---

# Operational Axioms for Terminal Work

---

**A1. The path is the product.**
Nothing scores except a file at the exact named location. Write to
it early — before analysis, before alternatives, before confidence.
A structurally minimal file at the right path earns more than a
complete solution that never lands.

**A2. Read everything once, in parallel.**
Open the instruction and every file it names in a single batch.
Know the exact output path, format, and what the assertions check
before touching anything else. Sequential reads of independent files
waste turns.

**A3. Observe; don't extrapolate.**
Write only values you have seen in the workspace. Inferred or
pattern-completed values fail exact-value checks identically to a
missing file. Where data cannot be recovered, write what you
verified — partial truth outscores invented completeness.

**A4. Run it; don't read it.**
Verification means execution. Invoke the runner the verifier will
use, from the directory it expects, with the flags it expects.
Compile immediately after writing compiled programs; read back the
output to confirm boundary bytes. A test file read but not run tells
you nothing.

**A5. Two failures means change the mechanism.**
If the same approach has failed twice, the third attempt must use a
structurally different mechanism. Adjusting parameters in a broken
approach is not a new attempt — it is the same failure repeated.

**A6. Match exactly.**
Path, filename, field names, function signatures, keywords, and
quoted literals must match the instruction character-for-character.
Numbers and size limits are requirements. The verifier does not
fuzzy-match.

**A7. Scripts are islands.**
A script that runs in a separate environment must install its own
dependencies, create its own directories, write its own
configurations, and start its own services. It must confirm each
service is reachable before exiting. Nothing from the current
session carries over.

**A8. Rewrite configs; don't patch them.**
Rewrite configuration files whole. Line-insertion tools require
structural knowledge of the host file that is easy to get wrong;
a full rewrite does not. Where drop-in directories exist, use them.

**A9. Copy before you parse.**
Copy any artifact before passing it to a recovery or parsing tool.
Some tools modify their input on open. Inspect raw bytes after
copying. For encoded data without a supplied key, derive the
transform from known-plaintext at a fixed offset.

**A10. Special characters need the file tool.**
Content with `$`, backticks, or nested quotes must be written with
the file tool. Shell heredocs silently corrupt these characters
without error output.

**A11. Finish with a read, not a narration.**
The last action must confirm the deliverable exists at its exact
path with valid content. Describing what you wrote is not equivalent
to writing it. The verifier reads the file system, not the session
transcript.
